page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 physical cores on a dedicated arm64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to physical cores, each of the 96 vCPUs runs on a dedicated physical core without multi-threading.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This high memory-to-core ratio supports memory-intensive workloads. There is no local NVMe or SSD storage bundled with this bare-metal instance, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The server does not include any graphics processing units, which limits its utility for hardware-accelerated machine learning or rendering tasks. Additionally, the arm64 architecture requires software compatibility verification compared to traditional x86 platforms.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the Graviton-based architecture offers a high density of dedicated physical cores and memory resources relative to its hardware class. This configuration is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated AWS Graviton physical cores on an arm64 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and graphics processing units, relying entirely on network-attached storage and focusing purely on CPU and memory-bound tasks. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This hardware profile provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The arm64 architecture requires compatible software stacks, making it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics pipelines.
